description: Machine-readable catalog of structured schema definitions and media file entries included in the SOPHIAS dataset. The catalog links each expected dataset file pattern to its corresponding JSON Schema or media-file description, supporting file validation, modality-specific interpretation, and reproducible reuse. Some files or modalities may be absent for specific presentation sessions due to participant consent restrictions or technical issues documented in the manuscript.

schemas.description: Session-level mapping file linking internal AICoFe presenter identifiers to presentation folder identifiers, together with participant demographic information such as gender and age.

Rubric-based assessment records collected through AICoFe for a given presenter. The file includes rubric item and section identifiers, teacher, peer, and self-assessment scores, and the corresponding free-text comments.

Timestamped contextual annotations recorded during the presentation session, including event labels, associated AICoFe participant identifiers when applicable, and start/end times in local and UNIX timestamp formats.

Presentation-level metadata describing the session, including participant roles, internal AICoFe identifiers, presentation timing information, and sensor or device assignment metadata when applicable.
Timestamped keyboard and clicker interaction events recorded during the presentation, including key presses used for slide navigation or other presenter interactions.
Timestamped mouse interaction events recorded during the presentation, including event type, screen coordinates, and local/UNIX timestamps for synchronization with other modalities.
Raw heart-rate signal collected from Fitbit Sense smartwatches through Watch-DMLT for a specific participant role in the session, such as presenter, peer evaluator, teacher, or observer.
Raw three-axis accelerometer signal collected from the Fitbit Sense smartwatch for a specific participant role, with timestamped motion measurements suitable for alignment with other modalities.
Raw three-axis gyroscope signal collected from the Fitbit Sense smartwatch for a specific participant role, providing timestamped rotational motion measurements.
Timestamped smartwatch orientation data for a specific participant role, describing the device orientation during the presentation session.
Frame-level facial bounding-box coordinates extracted from the presenter-view webcam video during the face-processing pipeline.
Frame-level head-pose estimates extracted from the presenter-view webcam video, including yaw, pitch, and roll angles for frames with valid face detections.
Frame-level facial landmark coordinates extracted from the presenter-view webcam video, including the five key facial landmarks returned by the face-detection pipeline.
Frame-level facial bounding-box coordinates extracted from the first-person eye-tracker scene video when available.
Frame-level head-pose estimates extracted from the eye-tracker scene video, including yaw, pitch, and roll angles for frames with valid face detections.
Frame-level facial landmark coordinates extracted from the eye-tracker scene video when face detections are available.

Gaze data stream recorded with Tobii Pro Glasses 3. The file is stored as JSON Lines, with one gaze sample per line, including gaze vectors, pupil-related measurements, validity information, and timestamps.
Inertial measurement unit data recorded with Tobii Pro Glasses 3. The file is stored as JSON Lines, with one IMU sample per line, including accelerometer and gyroscope measurements with timestamps.

Metadata describing the eye-tracker recording session, including recording duration, creation time, timezone, scene-camera information, gaze-stream metadata, event-stream metadata, and IMU-stream metadata.

media_files.description: Audience-facing classroom video captured through the edBB system, showing the evaluator and observer area when consent was provided.
Presenter-view classroom video captured through the edBB system, showing the student presentation from the frontal webcam perspective when consent was provided.
First-person scene video recorded with Tobii Pro Glasses 3 by the external observer during the presentation session.
Webcam-based audio recording captured locally through the edBB sound-capture system and aligned with the edBB audiovisual acquisition infrastructure.
Ambient audio recording captured through the Microsoft Teams session and synchronized with the edBB webcam-audio reference during preprocessing.
